If you're wondering how such a relatively low-profile matchup ended up as the most-watched ever on ESPN, a detailed explanation is below: www.sportsmediawatch.com/2026/07/wimb...

items.title: Halftime: Suns 53, Pistons 46.
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt:
Halftime: Suns 53, Pistons 46. 

Williams (12 pts, 4-7 ovr, 3 assts, 0 turns) and Jones (12 pts, 5-7 ovr) have done the heavy lifting. Okorie (8 pts, 3-7 ovr, 2 assts, 2 turns) and Lanier (8 pts, 2-6 from 3) helped power their late run to cut a 17-point deficit.

items.title: When you speak to someone about Chris Cenac Jr., those who know him rave about his work ethic & determination.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
When you speak to someone about Chris Cenac Jr., those who know him rave about his work ethic & determination.

His burning desire to be his best self is why he left home for Link Academy in MO. It's why he thrived under Kelvin Sampson. And it's why the Celtics are investing in his pursuit.
